stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Olms are a salamander species with two distinct populations.
  • Black olms live near the surface of streams and have darkly pigmented skin.
  • White olms live in deep underwater caves and have lost most of their skin pigmentation.
  • White olms have especially long, slender heads with many sensory pits along the sides.
  • These pits help white olms detect prey in the absence of light.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a difference between black olms and white olms. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Olms are a salamander species with two distinct populations: black olms and white olms.
Black olms have darkly pigmented skin, whereas white olms have lost most of their skin pigmentation.
Black olms and white olms may belong to the same species, but they are quite different.
White olms can no longer rely on sight and use sensory pits along their heads to detect prey.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a difference between black olms and white olms, noting that black olms have darkly pigmented skin while white olms have lost most of their skin pigmentation.

Choice A is incorrect. The sentence notes that olms have two distinct populations but doesn’t emphasize a specific difference between them. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence claims that the two populations are different, it doesn’t emphasize any particular difference. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes how white olms detect prey; it doesn’t emphasize a difference between the two populations.
